reasoning: As of May 7, 2025, evidence suggests that Starlink is highly likely to be available in Lesotho by July 1, 2025. Lesotho officially granted Starlink a 10-year operational license on April 14, 2025 (search_0_result_0). This removed the primary legal hurdle. In early 2025, Starlink demonstrated an aggressive expansion strategy in Africa, with Somalia (licensed April 13, 2025) and Niger (licensed March 2025) both seeing 'Available' status on the Starlink map almost immediately following their licensing (search_7_result_2, search_3_result_7). While an April 16 report indicated the map still showed '2025' for Lesotho (search_2_result_6), the phrase 'Lesotho gets Starlink' in local tech headlines from the same week (search_5_result_6) and the explicit public support from Lesotho’s Prime Minister (search_2_result_4) strongly point toward a rapid rollout. Given the typical 2-4 week lag seen in recent 2025 African launches and the fact that 11 weeks remain between licensing and the July 1 deadline, the probability is high. Only unexpected logistics or local interference would prevent the map status from flipping before July.
